# terrible_azure_azcollection_azure_rm_monitoractivitylogalerts (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the activity log alert you're creating, updating or deleting.
- `resource_group` (String) The name of the resource group

### Optional

- `actions` (String) The actions that will activate when the condition is met. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2020_10_01.models.actionlist?view=azure-python
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `condition` (String) The condition that will cause this alert to activate. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/python/api/azure-mgmt-monitor/azure.mgmt.monitor.v2020_10_01.models.alertruleallofcondition?view=azure-python
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) A description of this Activity Log Alert rule.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Indicates whether this activity log alert is enabled. If an activity log alert is not enabled, then none of its receivers will receive communications. Server side default false -- should be true. https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/issues/35343
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `location` (String) Location of the activity log alert. Should be optional, but is not optional on creation. https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/issues/35342 Activity log alerts are usually 'Global'. On creation module will default to 'Global' if unspecified.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `scopes` (String) A list of resource IDs that will be used as prefixes. The alert will only apply to Activity Log events with resource IDs that fall under one of these prefixes. This list must include at least one item for creation.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the activity log alert. Use `present` for creating/updating a activity log alert. Use `absent` for deleting a activity log alert.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `activitylogalert` (String) Details of the activity log alert Is null on state==absent (activity log alert does not exist or will be deleted) Assumes you make legal changes in check mode
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
